title: Ps 68698   more agressive speed ups
body: PS-68698 - Improve file write performance by using better buffer sizing on File Open and CopyTo methods.
author: rroesler

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR (PS-68698) targets faster mobile file upload throughput by switching the SyncController streaming endpoints to more async-friendly file I/O and larger buffers, along with a couple of packaging/SQL script updates that appear to be regeneration/cleanup artifacts.

**Changes:**
- Updated `SyncController` upload endpoints to use async `FileStream` with larger buffers, and disabled ASP.NET Core request size limits for those endpoints.
- Removed a no-longer-present override file entry from the deploy packer manifest.
- Regenerated/reordered parts of the SQL `delta_procs.sql` script (including tool metadata changes).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql | Regeneration/reordering of function drop/create blocks and updated sqlpackage metadata header. |
| Core/DeployScripts/Packer.manifest | Removed `Overrides\\MobileService-Web.config` from the package manifest list. |
| Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs | Upload streaming performance changes (async file I/O, larger buffers, request size limit removal) plus an `#if` notes block. |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2)</summary>

**Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs:2681**
* `[DisableRequestSizeLimit]` removes Kestrel’s request body cap, but this endpoint only validates `size` *after* streaming the entire request to disk. That enables very large/unbounded uploads (intentional or malicious) and can exhaust disk/IO before the post-write check runs; additionally, failed validation leaves the written file on disk because `RaiseErrorIfTrue` throws without cleanup. Consider enforcing a hard max (e.g., via `RequestSizeLimit` or `IHttpMaxRequestBodySizeFeature`) and validating `size` (positive + <= max) before writing; also delete the partially written file when validation fails.
```
        [DisableRequestSizeLimit] // file size is validated via the size parameter after upload
        public async Task<ProjectSightActionResult> UploadFile(Guid portfolioGuid, int projectId, string fileGuid, long size, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
        {
            // Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward

            await ConfigureAccessForPortfolioDBUserAsync(portfolioGuid, projectId, cancellationToken: cancellationToken);
```
**Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs:2829**
* `[DisableRequestSizeLimit]` removes the server-side body cap, but `offset`/`size` are only validated after the request body has been fully read and written. This allows very large/unbounded chunks that can consume disk/IO before the mismatch is detected, and failures will leave a partially written file behind. Please enforce an explicit maximum chunk size and validate `offset`/`size` (non-negative, no overflow in `offset + size`, <= max) before writing; also ensure partial files are cleaned up on validation failures.
```
        [DisableRequestSizeLimit] // chunk size is controlled by the client; the offset+size parameters validate the payload
        public async Task<ProjectSightActionResult> UploadAppend(Guid portfolioGuid, int projectId, string fileGuid, int block, long offset, long size, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
        {
            // Used in Mobile 2.4x and forward

            await ConfigureAccessForPortfolioDBUserAsync(portfolioGuid, projectId, cancellationToken: cancellationToken);

            // Get file to the server temp location
```
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: `delta_procs.sql` is a generated artifact (see `docs/ai/BACKEND_CONVENTIONS.md` §3: “Agents should not modify this file”). This hunk appears to be a regeneration that bakes in a developer-specific `C:\Users\...\sqlpackage.exe` path/version, creating noisy diffs and making future regenerations non-deterministic. Please avoid committing tool-environment metadata changes here; make any intentional proc changes in `base_procs.sql` and regenerate `delta_procs.sql` via the standard process so the output is stable.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Increasing the `CopyToAsync` buffer to 1 MB improves throughput but also allocates a 1 MB buffer per request (in addition to the `FileStream` buffer). Under high concurrency this can materially increase memory/GC pressure. Consider making the buffer size configurable, or using a pooled buffer/custom copy loop (e.g., `ArrayPool<byte>`) so concurrent uploads don’t allocate large transient arrays.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same as `UploadFile`: the 1 MB `CopyToAsync` buffer allocates a large per-request array. If many chunk uploads are in-flight concurrently, this can lead to high transient memory usage and GC churn. Consider a pooled-buffer copy loop or a smaller/tunable buffer size to balance throughput vs memory.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The `#if MORE_AI_WORK` block reads like internal notes/brainstorming and adds a large amount of dead code/comments to a production controller. Please remove this block (or move the content to an issue/wiki) before merging; if you want to keep a reminder in code, prefer a small `TODO(PS-68698): ...` with a link to the tracking item.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: There are existing integration tests for `SyncController` (e.g., `Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Mobile/SyncControllerTests.cs`), but none appear to cover the `uploadfile` / `uploadAppend` streaming endpoints. Since this change alters request-size handling and streaming behavior, please add an integration test that uploads a payload larger than the default Kestrel limit and asserts the server accepts it (and that the size/offset validation and cleanup behavior work as expected for mismatches).
review_comments.author: Copilot

+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates
+  @PortfolioId      uniqueidentifier,
+  @ProjectId        int,
+  @LastSyncDateTime datetimeoffset
+AS
+  SELECT CaptureGuid as UploadGuid,
+         Status as CaptureStatus,
+         Cast((CASE WHEN CaptureFileLocation IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS BIT) as Uploaded,
+         LastModified 
+    FROM SiteCaptureUploadRequests
+   WHERE PortfolioId = @PortfolioId
+     AND ProjectId = @ProjectID
+     AND LastModified >= @LastSyncDateTime
+
+   UNION
+   
+  SELECT CaptureGuid as UploadGuid,
+         Status as CaptureStatus,
+         Cast((CASE WHEN CaptureFileLocation IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS BIT) as Uploaded,
+         LastModified 
+    FROM SiteCaptureUploadRequestsArchive 
+   WHERE PortfolioId = @PortfolioId
+     AND ProjectId = @ProjectID
+     AND LastModified >= @LastSyncDateTime;
+
+  -- Consider indexes on PortfolioId, ProjectId, LastModified for both tables if this query is slow
+  -- and it is expected to return a small number of records based on the LastSyncDateTime filter 
+GO

+﻿USE ProjectSight
+GO
+SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
+GO
+/* Core Update Script */
+
+  /* AT THE START OF EACH RELEASE: ************************
+
+    0. See 0ReadMe.txt for a more complete description
+
+    1. Copy this file to a file for the new version.  For example when going from version 10.6 to 10.7, create a
+       copy named update_db_10.7.sql.
+    2. Add the copy to the VS.NET SQL Project.
+    3. In the new copy, remove the lines of script between the lines "BEGINNING OF UPDATES ..." and "END OF UPDATES ..."
+    4. * NEW * DO NOT Remove the old update_db_xx.yy.sql file (e.g. update_db.10.6)
+       from the VS.NET project BUT keep in Source Control (TFS).
+    -  * NEW * old patch files can be removed if their content has been merged into the update_db for the next release
+    5. Update the version number on line the "UPDATE versions" statement near the end of the file
+    6. Update the version number in base_tables.sql (the "INSERT INTO versions" at the end of the file)
+    7. Update the version number in base_procs.sql (the "INSERT INTO versions" at the end of the file)
+    8. In ManifestUpdate.txt: update the reference to this file name to be the new file name, and update the version number on the same line.
+    9. Update the installer to include the new file.
+
+    *. Search for "DB VERSION NUMBER" -- locations where version information might need to be updated
+
+    See the Branch/Builds document for an overview: https://goo.gl/HkamLq (old https://goo.gl/eWiKcu).
+  */
+
+  /* Add a utility proc for dropping default constraints */
+IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.s_util_drop_default') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+GO
+
+/****** Object: Stored Procedure s_util_drop_default ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+  @table_name     sysname ,
+  @column_name    sysname ,
+  @no_message     bit = 0
+AS
+DECLARE
+  @default_name   sysname ,
+  @table_id       int
+
+  SELECT @default_name = s.name
+    FROM (syscolumns sc INNER JOIN sysobjects s ON sc.cdefault = s.id)
+         INNER JOIN sysobjects pt ON sc.id = pt.id
+   WHERE sc.name = @column_name
+     AND pt.name = @table_name
+
+  IF @default_name IS NOT NULL
+    BEGIN
+      IF @no_message = 0
+        PRINT 'Dropping constraint ' + @default_name + ' from table ' + @table_name
+
+      EXEC('ALTER TABLE dbo.' + @table_name + ' DROP CONSTRAINT ' + @default_name)
+    END
+
+GO
+
+IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#update') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP TABLE #update
+GO
+CREATE TABLE #update
+(
+    update_data          BIT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
+)
+GO
+DELETE FROM #update
+INSERT INTO #update VALUES(0)
+GO
+
+IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.objects WHERE type = 'FN' AND name = 'db_less_than')
+   DROP FUNCTION dbo.db_less_than
+GO
+
+CREATE FUNCTION dbo.db_less_than(@program nvarchar(50),@major int,@minor int,@revision int) RETURNS bit
+AS
+BEGIN
+   DECLARE @result bit
+
+   IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM versions WHERE Program_ID = @program AND (Major < @major OR (Major = @major AND Minor < @minor) OR (MAJOR = @major AND Minor = @minor AND revision < @revision)))
+       SET @result = 1
+   ELSE
+       SET @result = 0
+
+   RETURN (@result)
+END
+GO
+
+/* Make sure we have a version number */
+IF NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM versions WHERE program_id = 'Core DB')
+  INSERT INTO versions (program_id, major, minor, revision) VALUES ('Core DB', 0, 0, 0)
+GO
+
+/******************************** PREPARE FOR UPDATES *************************************/
+
+IF EXISTS( SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE name = DB_NAME() and compatibility_level < 120  )

+USE IgnitionMaster
+GO
+SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
+GO
+  /* Add a utility proc for dropping default constraints */
+IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.s_util_drop_default') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+GO
+/****** Object: Stored Procedure s_util_drop_default ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+  @table_name     sysname ,
+  @column_name    sysname ,
+  @no_message     bit = 0
+AS
+DECLARE
+  @default_name   sysname ,
+  @table_id       int
+
+  SELECT @default_name = s.name
+    FROM (syscolumns sc INNER JOIN sysobjects s ON sc.cdefault = s.id)
+         INNER JOIN sysobjects pt ON sc.id = pt.id
+   WHERE sc.name = @column_name
+     AND pt.name = @table_name
+
+  IF @default_name IS NOT NULL
+    BEGIN
+      IF @no_message = 0
+        PRINT 'Dropping constraint ' + @default_name + ' from table ' + @table_name
+
+      EXEC('ALTER TABLE dbo.' + @table_name + ' DROP CONSTRAINT ' + @default_name)
+    END
+GO
+
+  /* Add a utility proc for getting the current environment */
+IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.fn_util_get_env') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP FUNCTION dbo.fn_util_get_env
+GO
+/****** Object: Function fn_util_get_env ******/
+CREATE FUNCTION dbo.fn_util_get_env() RETURNS NVARCHAR(100)
+AS
+BEGIN
+DECLARE @environment nvarchar(100)
+
+  SELECT @environment =
+    CASE Environment
+    WHEN 'projectsight-prod' THEN 'na'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-prodeu' THEN 'eu'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-prodau' THEN 'au'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-produk' THEN 'uk'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-ps-gvi-p-sql-0' THEN 'hp'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-fea1' THEN 'fea1'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-fea2' THEN 'fea2'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-fea3' THEN 'fea3'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-fea4' THEN 'fea4'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-UAT' THEN 'fea5'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-fea6' THEN 'fea6'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-qa' THEN 'qa'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-performance' THEN 'performance'
+    WHEN 'projectsight-staging' THEN 'staging'
+    ELSE CASE WHEN TIDCallbackURL like 'http://localhost%' THEN 'localhost' ELSE 'other' END
+    END
+    FROM Control
+
+  RETURN @Environment
+END
+GO
+
+IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#update') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP TABLE #update
+GO
+CREATE TABLE #update
+(
+    update_data          BIT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
+)
+GO
+DELETE FROM #update
+INSERT INTO #update VALUES(0)
+GO
+
+/******************************** BEGINNING OF UPDATES FOR THIS VERSION *************************************/
+
+DECLARE @environment nvarchar(100)
+SELECT @environment = dbo.fn_util_get_env()
+
+UPDATE ExternalService 
+   SET ParametersJson = '{"Region":"' + UPPER(@environment) + '"}'
+WHERE Type = 201 -- GainSight - Tracking Key
+  AND (ParametersJson IS NULL OR ParametersJson = '');
